stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta's Llama 3 8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP topics including 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The mention of 'safetensors' indicates the use of modern, efficient model serialization formats.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags such as 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The focus is on a specific, powerful open-source LLM variant from Meta.

stories.title: Anthropic says Alibaba must be punished for largest Claude cloning attack
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Anthropic accuses Alibaba of a large-scale attack involving the cloning of its Claude AI model.
The alleged attack involved the use of 25,000 accounts to generate over 28.8 million exchanges with Claude.
The incident highlights growing concerns over AI data scraping, misuse, and intellectual property protection in the LLM space.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes key ML technologies such as transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
The associated tags (transformers, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern NLP/AI ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads on Apple hardware.
This is presented as a preview, indicating an ongoing optimization and development effort.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Notion killing Skiff-influenced email app since most users use AI agents instead
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Notion is heavily integrating AI agents to manage user inboxes.
This move suggests a shift where AI agents are replacing traditional email application functionality.
The development is positioned as a response to the growing adoption of AI agents across productivity tools.

stories.title: When Does Combining Language Models Help? A Co-Failure Ceiling on Routing, Voting, and Mixture-of-Agents Across 67 Frontier Models
stories.author: Josef Chen
bullet_points: The paper analyzes the performance limits of multi-model LLM systems (routing, voting, etc.) designed to improve accuracy over single models.
It introduces a theoretical 'Co-Failure Ceiling' demonstrating that accuracy gain is fundamentally capped by the rate at which all constituent models fail on the same query (beta).
The study highlights a limitation in current diagnostic methods, noting that standard error correlation (rho) cannot accurately identify this critical failure rate (beta).

stories.title: Prompt Injection in Automated Résumé Screening with Large Language Models: Single and Multi-Injection Settings
stories.author: Stefanus Jasin
bullet_points: The study investigates prompt injection attacks specifically within LLM-based automated résumé screening systems.
Prompt injection is defined as subtle, self-promotional text designed to manipulate LLM evaluations without adding new qualifications.
Experiments demonstrate that prompt injection reliably improves applicant rankings when résumé quality among candidates is similar.

stories.title: Reinforcement Learning without Ground-Truth Solutions can Improve LLMs
stories.author: Yuxiong He
bullet_points: Introduces RiVER, a new framework for training Large Language Models (LLMs) using Reinforcement Learning without requiring ground-truth solutions.
RiVER utilizes deterministic execution feedback as continuous-valued supervision, addressing a major limitation in traditional RLVR methods.
This approach expands the applicability of RL to complex tasks where definitive correct answers are unknown.

stories.title: Hallucination in World Models is Predictable and Preventable
stories.author: Xiaolong Wang
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of 'hallucination' in generative world models, where simulated futures drift from reality.
Proposes that hallucinations are predictable and concentrated in low-coverage areas of the state-action space.
Introduces MMBench2, a large-scale dataset designed to test and guide the mitigation of these model errors.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has significantly improved performance on Apple Silicon using its MLX engine.
The update results in faster response times and higher quality model outputs.
Memory usage for running models has been reduced with this optimization.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is expanding its capabilities to support local image generation.
The feature is currently experimental and available on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: YouTube updates Shorts to make it even more like TikTok
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: YouTube is integrating more TikTok-like features into its Shorts format.
A new 'clear screen' mode is being introduced to remove on-screen icons and text.
The traditional 'thumbs-up' button is being replaced with a 'heart' icon for engagement.

stories.title: OpenAI will delay GPT-5.6 after Trump administration request
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: OpenAI is reportedly delaying the full release of GPT-5.6.
The delay is allegedly due to a request from the Trump administration citing security concerns.
OpenAI plans to initially release GPT-5.6 in a limited preview form.

stories.title: Instagram wants to monopolize your attention
stories.author: Charles Pulliam-Moore
bullet_points: Instagram is expanding its presence on smart TVs with new features for its TV app.
The primary goal of these new features is to increase user engagement and time spent on the platform.
The features, including vertical Reels, are currently available on platforms like Amazon Fire TV and Google TV.

stories.title: FCC may kill $2B program that connects schools and libraries to Internet
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: The FCC is reportedly considering terminating a $2 billion program designed to provide internet access to schools and libraries.
The cancellation is linked to concerns raised by Carr regarding excessive screen time among students.
The controversy frames the issue as a debate over regulatory overreach versus digital equity.

stories.title: Feds deny Polestar authorization to sell cars in US from model year 2027
stories.author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
bullet_points: Federal authorities have denied Polestar authorization to sell vehicles in the United States.
This denial specifically affects models starting from the 2027 model year.
The situation contrasts with Volvo, which has maintained authorization for sales in the US.

stories.title: New effort will get genome sequences for entire Endangered Species list
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: Colossal Biosciences is undertaking a new initiative to sequence the genomes of all endangered species.
The project involves biobanking tissues from these endangered species.
This effort represents a significant application of advanced genomics and biobanking technology for conservation.

stories.title: Language-Based Digital Twins for Elderly Cognitive Assistance
stories.author: Hiroko H. Dodge
bullet_points: Proposes a language-based digital twin framework for personalized healthcare.
Leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) to mimic elderly individuals' conversational behavior.
Aims to use conversational patterns as non-invasive biomarkers for early detection of Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI).

stories.title: Empowering GUI Agents via Autonomous Experience Exploration and Hindsight Experience Utilization for Task Planning
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: The research addresses limitations in small, efficient Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) used for GUI agent task planning.
A new method, Planning Experience Exploration and Utilization (PEEU), is introduced to improve autonomous exploration and task decomposition.
The work focuses on enabling small, open-source agents to handle complex, repetitive GUI tasks across different websites.

stories.title: AI Healthcare Chatbots as Information Infrastructure: A Large-Scale Study of User-Reported Breakdowns
stories.author: Masooda Bashir
bullet_points: The study analyzes over 15,000 user reviews across 59 AI healthcare chatbots to assess real-world performance.
Three major recurring breakdowns were identified: access barriers/service unreliability, and user experience/interaction quality.
The research highlights the need for further study on the impact and function of AI chatbots in health information seeking.

stories.title: DanceOPD: On-Policy Generative Field Distillation
stories.author: Tat-Seng Chua
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of unifying diverse capabilities (T2I, local/global editing) in modern image generation models.
Identifies a core problem where editing capabilities often degrade or conflict with base text-to-image performance.
Proposes 'DanceOPD' as a new method to effectively compose and align these conflicting image generation functionalities.

stories.title: When are likely answers right? On Sequence Probability and Correctness in LLMs
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: The paper investigates the relationship between sequence probability (as calculated by LLMs) and actual correctness in generated outputs.
It analyzes how different LLM decoding methods shift probability mass, questioning when this probabilistic likelihood aligns with factual accuracy.
The research aims to quantify this alignment across various decoding strategies and models.

stories.title: Error-Conditioned Neural Solvers
stories.author: Jeong Joon Park
bullet_points: Neural surrogate models for solving PDEs struggle with constraint violations and extrapolation beyond training data.
Existing hybrid methods improve physical correctness by targeting PDE residuals but suffer from high computational cost.
The research proposes a novel approach to address the limitations of purely statistical neural solvers in physical simulations.

stories.title: Empowering GUI Agents via Autonomous Experience Exploration and Hindsight Experience Utilization for Task Planning
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: The research addresses limitations in small, open-source Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) used for GUI agent task planning.
It introduces the Planning Experience Exploration and Utilization (PEEU) method to improve autonomous exploration and task decomposition.
The work aims to enhance the ability of cost-efficient agents to handle complex, repetitive GUI tasks across different websites.

stories.title: DanceOPD: On-Policy Generative Field Distillation
stories.author: Tat-Seng Chua
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of unifying diverse capabilities (T2I, local/global editing) in modern image generation models.
A key problem identified is the inherent conflict between these capabilities, where editing often degrades base T2I performance.
The proposed solution, DanceOPD, is introduced to effectively compose and align these conflicting image generation functionalities.

stories.title: Empowering GUI Agents via Autonomous Experience Exploration and Hindsight Experience Utilization for Task Planning
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: The research addresses the weak planning and limited generalization of small, cost-efficient Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) used for GUI agents.
It introduces the Planning Experience Exploration and Utilization (PEEU) method to enhance autonomous task planning for repetitive GUI operations.
The work focuses on enabling small, open-source MLLMs to effectively decompose complex tasks into executable actions across different websites.

stories.title: Beyond Surface Forms: A Comprehensive, Mechanism-Oriented Taxonomy of Indirect Linguistic Encoding for LLM-Based Coded Language Detection
stories.author: Paul Jen-Hwa Hu
bullet_points: The research addresses the problem of users employing Indirect Linguistic Encoding (ILE) to evade content moderation and surveillance on social media.
It proposes a mechanism-oriented taxonomy to categorize these evasive linguistic expressions (like algospeak and euphemisms) based on their underlying encoding operations.
This work is relevant to improving the detection capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) against adversarial obfuscation.

stories.title: How Surprising Is Historical Italian to Language Models? Tokenization Tax, Comprehension Tax, and a Simple Mitigation
stories.author: Maria Levchenko
bullet_points: The paper addresses the poorly understood challenge of Large Language Models (LLMs) processing historical languages, specifically Italian.
It introduces a diagnostic framework to break down historical language difficulty into four distinct dimensions, moving beyond a monolithic view.
These dimensions include tokenization cost, predictive uncertainty (surprisal), and semantic robustness, suggesting potential mitigation strategies.

stories.title: LMs as Task-Specific Knowledge Bases: An Interpretability Analysis
stories.author: Mor Geva
bullet_points: The paper investigates the hypothesis that Large Language Models (LMs) function as task-specific knowledge bases.
A core property of knowledge bases (consistency across queries) is tested against LMs using behavioral and mechanistic analyses.
Findings indicate that LMs encode factual knowledge in a manner that is dependent on the specific task being performed.

stories.title: Functional doesn't mean correct. That's the biggest risk with AI-generated code.
stories.author: cyclopt_dimitrisk
bullet_points: AI-generated code can execute successfully without being functionally correct.
The primary risk highlighted is the difference between code running and code being correct.
The article discusses failure modes inherent in relying on AI for code generation.

stories.title: Cognitive Pong: An Open Source Arena Where AI Agents Compete, Learn, and Train
stories.author: bclonan
bullet_points: Introduces 'Cognitive Pong,' an open-source platform for AI agent competition.
The core hypothesis is that structured competition improves AI reasoning and training data quality.
The project aims to advance AI model capabilities through self-play and competitive learning.
